The Canada lynx, a rare wild cat, has been reported in Vermont for the first time in six years,  according to a press release from the Vermont Fish and Wildlife Department.  The lynx was seen walking along a road in Rutland County on Aug. 17 and was recorded on video by several residents, said Brehan Furfey, a wildlife biologist with the department, in the Wednesday press release. Canada lynx are endangered in Vermont and threatened — meaning likely to become endangered — in the United States. Their endangered status has made lynx rare to spot in Vermont. Read more from Emma Malinak at vtdigger.org. Video courtesy of Gary Shattuck.

Every Sunday from 9 AM – 3 PM, the backside of the beautiful and peaceful Thai Buddhist Temple of Dallas  transforms into a vibrant market with dozens of food stalls. Temple members volunteer to cook up all kinds of authentic Thai dishes, with all proceeds supporting the temple. This longtime tradition is still going strong, but some stalls sell out before noon, so pro tip: arrive early! Another pro tip: it’s CASH ONLY! 💵 FOODS TO TRY 🥭 Mango Sticky Rice 🌶️ Papaya Salad 🥓 Pork Skewers ☕️ Thai Coffee 🌿 Basil Chicken 🌭 Spicy Sausage 🍍 Pineapple Rice 🍜 Boat Noodles 🐟 Fish Cakes  Grab a table next to the market, or walk to the Temple gardens for a shaded, peaceful spot to eat. There’s even a Koi Pond to enjoy!   You may see monks walking the grounds, and you’re welcome to step inside to admire the stunning art. If you’re interested in learning more about Buddhism, the Temple hosts a Western discussion in English every Wednesday at 7 PM, following chanting & meditation.  While the market is open to the community, please remember to be respectful of the Temple grounds by dressing modestly, being quiet, treating the monks with respect and refrain from PDA.  📍 8484 Stults Rd, Dallas, TX 75243

Three disappearances in the span of two years..  It’s been two years since 5-year-old Summer Wells vanished without a trace from a small town in Tennessee. Jennifer Santanello, the mother of Layla who was last seen on June 27, said she found out that Layla and Snapp knew a lot of the same people. Snapp was last seen on Oct. 15 on Bell Ridge Drive in Kingsport. Jennifer said she also noticed that both girls had a similar height and weight. “The similarities in measurements strikes me as scary,” Jennifer said. “It could mean so many things.” Snapp’s former foster mom, Leslie Hunt, contacted Jennifer when she noticed the connection between the cases. On June 15, 2021, Summer’s parents said she was helping her mother and grandmother plant flowers before going inside their Rogersville home to play with her toys.  Then, out of nowhere, Summer vanished. That was the last time her mother said she had seen her daughter. “I yelled downstairs for her a couple of times, and I didn’t get an answer. Which was unusual because usually she always answers me, so I went down there to check and she was nowhere inside, she was just gone,” Summer Wells’ mother Candus Wells said. Her family believes Summer may have been abducted, taken down a steep hill behind the home — possibly to a waiting car along the road. #summerwells #summerwellsmissing #summerwellsupdates #summerwellsmissingupdates #summerwellsupdate #canduswells #drphil #drphilshow  #truecrime #truecrimecommunity #crimetok #murdermystery #murder #murderstory #murdermystery2 #breakingnews #news #complex #latestnews #trending #trendingreels #netflix #documentary #documentaries #serialkiller #serialkillers #fyp #tiktok #fypage #viral #viralvideo #viraltiktok #hulu #trendingvideo #trendingtiktok #missinggirl #drphil #drphilshow #nancygrace #nancygraceonthescene #scary #scarystories #scarytiktoks #scaryvideos #xybca #xyzbcafypシ

Another wrong way fatal crash on the 10 freeway.  A wrong-way crash that claimed at least one life led to the 10 Freeway being shut down in downtown Los Angeles early on Easter Sunday.  Preliminary information from the California Highway Patrol indicates that officers were called to the westbound span of the 10 at Central Avenue for the wrong-way collision at 5:25 a.m.  A total of three vehicles were involved, CHP confirmed. Stretch marks are scars caused by broken collagen and elastin. They are very common in women and can form during growth spurts in puberty or pregnancy, and can appear red, purple or white. It is hard to completely get rid of stretch marks, but there are treatments you can try at home to reduce the appearance of both new and old stretch marks. Studies show that hyaluronic acid, retinoids like tretinoin and moisturizers containing centella asiatica like Cicalfate or Cicaplast can help reduce their appearance at home or prevent them from forming (skip the retinoid if pregnant). #stretchmarks #skincareroutine #fyp
